text:
A novel organic hybrid molecule, 4-(((4-ethyl-5-(thiophen-2-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazol-3-yl)thio)methyl)-6,7-dimethyl-2H-chromen-2-one (1), was synthesized by reaction of thiophen-substituted triazole-thiol with chloromethyl-substituted dimethylcoumarin. The structure of the compound was confirmed by means of elemental analysis, IR and multi-nuclear ($^{1}$H and $^{13}$C APT) NMR spectroscopy. Using Density Functional Theory (DFT) at the B3LYP/cc-pVDZ level, gas-phase structure and electronic properties of compound 1 were investigated and reactive sites were identified by molecular electrostatic potential (MEP) mapping and global reactivity descriptors.

In silico ADMET and toxicity evaluation of thiazolidinedione-(1H)-pyrazole hybrid and its assessment as SARS-CoV-2 inhibitor Abstract Herein we report the in silico pharmacological studies of thiazolidinedione-(1H)-pyrazole hybrid (TPH). The drug like properties were evaluated in terms of absorption of the drug, blood brain barrier penetration, acute toxicity classification and other toxicity parameters of the drug candidate in human body. The results indicated that the hybrid showed good absorption of drug, lacking penetration of blood brain barrier and fell under toxicity class 4 (LD50 ~1 g/kg) thereby indicating safety. Thus, we evaluated the therapeutic potential of the hybrid against SARS-CoV-2 by using the model of molecular docking and molecular dynamics (MD) simulations studies on the various crucial proteins of the virus i.e., Main Protease (Mpro), Nsp3, PLpro and Nsp9 of SARS-CoV-2. The hybrid was found to interact strongly in all the proteins tested by docking studies and the binding energies were found to be higher than the standard known drugs. Moreover the binding energy of hybrid was found to be maximum with Papain-like protease (PLpro). Stability of the complexes formed between protein and hybrid were further evaluated by MD simulations studies carried out for 50ns on all the proteins and the results showed stability of the complexes with Mpro, PLpro and Nsp3.
